title: NOC 41403 – Social Policy Researchers, Consultants and Program Officers | GTR Immigration
description: Social Policy Researchers, Consultants and Program Officers under NOC 41403 are professionals who design, analyze, and implement social policies and public programs across Canada. Classified under TEER 1 (Professional Occupation), this role plays a critical part in shaping social development, housing, employment equity, immigration, community planning, and international development strategies. These professionals work in government departments, NGOs, research institutions, hospitals, educational organizations, consulting firms, and international agencies.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category4 – Occupations in education, law, and social, community and government servicesTEER1 – Occupations usually require a university degreeMajor Group41 – Professional occupations in law, education, social, community and government servicesSub-Major Group414 – Professional occupations in government servicesMinor Group4140 – Policy and program researchers, consultants and officersNOC Code41403

common_job_titles: International aid and development project officer
duties: Social Policy Researchers and Program Officers may perform:
Develop and evaluate social programs and legislation
Conduct demographic, social, and economic research
Assess demographic and economic impacts on housing
Prepare and submit immigration and visa applications
Represent clients before administrative bodies(Note: Regulated consultants must meet licensing requirements.)
Plan and administer foreign aid programs
Assess impact of transportation and housing projects
employment_requirements: Bachelor’s degree in:Social sciencesPublic administrationBusiness administrationRelated discipline
Master’s degree may be required for senior roles
Home economists require a related specialized degree
Registration is required for regulated immigration consultants
Professional designation "Professional Home Economist" regulated in some provinces
Foreign-trained professionals may require credential assessment for immigration purposes.
salary_prospects: Social Policy Researchers and Program Officers typically earn between:
$44,300 – $104,100 per year,depending on specialization, experience, and sector.
Senior policy advisors and international development officers may earn significantly more.
With 52,700 projected job openings, NOC 41403 demonstrates strong employment potential due to:
This occupation offers long-term stability and public-sector growth.
labour_market_demand: Professionals under NOC 41403 are employed in:
This occupation shows strong long-term demand due to:

Eligibility for Express Entry and Provincial Nominee Programs

Professionals with experience in social research, immigration consulting, housing policy, or community development are particularly competitive.
pathways: Professionals under this NOC may qualify for:
Strong educational credentials and policy experience improve immigration competitiveness.
